title: How to treat a second smile line with Botox & Filler.
filename: How to treat a second smile line with Botox & Filler..en.vtt
excerpt: How do you treat a second smile so if you're anything like me you might be picturing this but it's not like that it's actually a little bit more subtle it's like this and this line upsets a minority of patients most of the lines that our patients complain about are vertical lip l…

segments.text: how do you treat a second smile so if you're anything like me you might be picturing this but it's not like that it's actually a little bit more subtle it's like this and this line upsets a minority of patients most of the lines that our patients complain about are vertical lip lines but there are a minority of patients who get a horizontal lip Line This lip crease troubles some of them whereas others actually find it quite endearing and they like that particular line our job as clinicians is to try and identify who will benefit from treatment and who will not and make sure we get the best result both psychosocially as well as aesthetically so in this episode we're going to discuss this line in depth we want to know who we should treat who we shouldn't treat how to treat it and why it can be quite tricky to get a good result on some patients let's now consider the anatomy that's driving the formation of thease the key thing to understand is that is caused by the muscles around the nose and the upper lip and I think this is actually quite variable between different patients so for example sometimes in some

patients it will be caused predominantly by the action of the depressor septi muscle and its Connections in that area of the face in other patients it may be the orbicular asaurus and I think the LI toi leasi plays a role in some patients and then in many patients it can be any of the lip elevators I think this is particularly true when a nose is quite strong you'll often notice a stronger nose with good lip elevation and you're very likely to develop a crease which means all the lip elevators may be playing a role so it can be zygomaticus major minor latal Lai muscle the levat Lai leasi muscle and the depress accepti muscle could all be contributing to the formation of this crease and you can see how this could be very difficult to treat because you cannot neutralize all of those muscles so in terms of who to treat we're actually looking for patients who are just like in many of our treatments somewhere in the middle of the spectrum if you got a very powerful lip elevation and a strong nose you're unlikely to be able to stop that with Botox and dermal filler however if it is milder and the crease is smaller

and the muscles involved tend to be a little bit on the weaker side but still enough to cause a crease you've got more of a chance of improving the situation so I have an example here Lea happens to have an upper lip crease and she was very interested when I said that this might be a problem for some people because she's actually always like the crease and this is very important to notice because removing something that a patient likes is never a good thing to do as a clinician I would actually never treat Lila's upper lip line crease until she becomes bothered by it if she does when she's much older in the short term we can use her face to understand the anatomy of what is driving this particular issue so Lea can you do a big smile as if I said a my usual hilarious joke okay have a look at what's happening as she's smiling we've got obviously action of zygomaticus major levor Lai Eli we've treated gummy smile in the past which is probably it is slightly softer than it was once do you think it's still working I think it's still working so in particular I see this pulling up here and then there's a rotation here and a very small formation of a crease and this is caused I would

say largely by the more medial lip elevators rather than zygomaticus major but you will see some patients with a big bigger nose and usually a broader smile where they're pulling up a lot laterally and they also get a crease it's probably harder to treat you probably could if you really wanted to soften it further with increasing the amount of toxin we've used in leval Lai muscle I would guess and it might work doing similar injections to a lip flip you just basically slightly neutralize some of this muscle which is curling up the lip because if you have a look a lot of what's Happening Here we've got levor labii which often curls round it affects the nostril but also curls into the lips so it's actually pulling the surface here but you also have I think in some people this action of this medial muscles that pull that pulls the surface of the lip up so you can imagine the downside of treating this crease might also be that she actually has less upper lip show and I think that's actually what you like about it is that your lip rotates rolls upwards so as you smile your lips get bigger M um and that would be the downside of completely

stopping this is if this no longer contracts and rotates upwards yes you get rid of the crease but you also lose the show of the top lip so that could be a really good reason not to treat this crease so how do we treat this problem you can treat this in multiple different ways and with different modalities like any crease in the face it's a combination of what is happening to the tissue and the effects of the muscle on the tissue so often we would think first to treat the muscles CA cusing it so how can we relax muscles in certain parts of the face without disharmonize if that's what the patient perceives now this can be done with very small amounts of boty toxin but it can also be done in some patients with dermal filler acts like a mechanical block of the muscle as you add volume the muscle has to contract harder against thermop fill and this is a common way that you can treat a gummy smile in fact in many cases I find it a little bit more easy to create a harmonious smile because mechanical blockage is a little bit gentler and

it's particularly useful when the patient already has some volume loss so if you have a patient with a horizontal lip crease and nasal abial fold and volume loss generally you should definitely be considering some dermal filler in the picture and this can start with nasal aial folds that decrease the lip elevators activity you can also do this with botam toxin of course and then you can also strengthen the lip with dermal filler now it's worth remembering that if you volumize a lip this actually has the opposite effect and you'll often see young girls unfortunately who've been overtreated who develop new creases because of the impact of having a large amount of dermal filler right next to tissue that hasn't got any additional strength involved so a strong lip Rises up and compresses tissue above and they develop an upper lip line crease or even more commonly the vertical crease that makes them look like a duck where you get a crease here where the cheek meets with the upper lip and that's caused by a similar effect which is you have a large amount of product next to an area that hasn't been treated creating a new

Focus for weakness so we need to do the opposite of that for example we can treat the filter columns in some patients so the columns are usually what becomes disrupted by this crease as the lip Rises up the tissue bends and the filter columns are dissected by the crease so you can get some benefit from directly treating The filtral Columns of course this fill is not massively powerful and it works it's better to do this in conjunction with other treatments but it can soften the crease in some patients this is a better course of action in most people than directly filling the crease so if you directly treat the horizontal line with filler this can create various disharmonies which we don't want the most important of which is you start to make the white lip compete with the pink lip so you now have a projecting white lip next to a relatively smaller pink lip it simply doesn't look natural you can do it a little bit tiny amounts of a low viscosity product might soften the line just enough in some patients but you really have to Rin yourself in from trying too hard or you will alter the

shape and create that kind of homo Simpson shape which we are often trying to avoid when treating upper lip lines overall I would make sure you select your patients carefully choose those with smaller lines and be completely honest with those with deep lines strong noses and big elevators that it's going to be very hard to soften this line if you enjoyed that and you like learning more about Anatomy make sure you sign up to my wait list we've got something really cool coming if you want to really Master this [Music] topic
